rules.description: Buttons should typically be translated as verbs, generally using the infinitive form
rules.notes: Be concise, avoid using articles or prepositions and go straight to the point

rules.description: Translations of buttons do not end in a period, unless they consist of more than one sentence
Make sure to capitalize only the first word of the button

As a rule, do NOT use any quotation marks around buttons. Only use quotation marks around button names to indicate where the actual button text ends, if the sentence is otherwise likely to confuse the user
rules.example_correct: Aloita napsauttamalla alla olevaa Tarkista ja siirry -painiketta

rules.section: Software Localization - Names of RingCentral Products, Applications and Features
rules.description: Product/application names are often trademarked, thus not to be localized. Check reference material and linguistic instructions, including terminology lists before translating

rules.section: Software Localization - Adaptation to Finnish Market
rules.description: Replace English examples with Finnish equivalents when needed for localization/adaptation
rules.notes: Example given was wildcard operators explanation

rules.description: Use informal addressing of people: sinä (you) instead of overly polite Te (You)
rules.notes: Target audience are businesses so the style should be appropriate for business

rules.description: Register should be professional and welcoming. Make it business casual. Be precise and clear, consistent and natural
rules.notes: Rephrase sentences that are not clear enough, and rework sentences that sound like a translation

rules.description: Avoid unnecessarily formal or pompous tone. Refrain from using words that add formality without adding meaning
Content should be accurate and concise (exact): no ambiguities or mistranslations
rules.notes: For most product UI, Support Content and Customer Training Materials

rules.description: Content should be clear and coherent (readable): target reader should understand the text easily
Content should be objective: neutral, do not insert your own opinion, avoid colloquial or slang words, be always politically correct, aim for bias-free language
Content should be correct: content and numbers should be free of mistakes
Content should be complete: do not leave out content (words, phrases, whole paragraphs)

Avoid a condescending, childish or overly familiar tone and treat the reader as an adult and a professional. Strive for clarity without resorting to unnecessary repetition. Downplay humor and keep the tone business-like
rules.notes: The tone of American documentation is often paternalistic

rules.description: Content should be idiomatically correct: use of correct verbs, metaphors, expressions, etc. so that text does not sound like a translation
rules.notes: For Marketing Content, E-mails, Product Guides, PR letters, Customer Proposals and selected UI components

rules.description: Avoid literal translations. Translating literally without paying attention to Finnish grammar and style can result in sentences that are hard to understand. Try to balance faithfulness to the source and readability in the target language
Avoid passive voice, where appropriate. Use active voice to improve readability. Passive voice is only advised in specific texts like legal and official documents
rules.notes: e.g. privacy policies, terms of a contract, NDAs, etc.

rules.description: Possessive forms should be used sparingly. Where possible, remove possessive pronouns and only use possessive suffixes instead
rules.notes: Possessive attributes are typically used frequently in English

rules.description: Internal cross-references mention other parts of text within the file or document you are translating. Translation of document titles must be referenced consistently throughout your translation

External cross-references mention titles of other documents. Check whether this text has been localized before, and match the relevant translations. If the document has not been translated before, leave as in source language
rules.notes: Contact Project Management team or Language Lead if unable to retrieve information

rules.description: Finnish does not require similar approach to English politeness. Do not hesitate to be more direct, and use the imperative form or similar constructions where appropriate
rules.notes: English may sometimes tend to be over polite with instructions beginning with Please

rules.description: English third person singular pronoun they (to avoid gendered pronouns) should be translated with third person singular hän using a singular verb
rules.example_correct: Asiakas ottaa yhteyttä palvelutiimiin. Hän voi myös soittaa yhteyskeskukseen
rules.example_incorrect: Asiakas ottaa yhteyttä palvelutiimiin. He voivat myös soittaa yhteyskeskukseen
rules.notes: Finnish does not have gendered pronouns

rules.description: Avoid bloated expressions
rules.notes: For most product UI, Support Content and Customer Training Materials

rules.description: Avoid overly long or complex compounds. Unintuitive compounds are ultimately an intelligibility and usability issue
Please is not translated. Translate sorry as valitettavasti, if needed due to context
rules.notes: For Marketing Content, E-mails, Product Guides, PR letters, Customer Proposals and selected UI components

rules.description: Translating Successfully is often not necessary
Avoid beginning sentences with Kuitenkin
Overtranslation should be avoided. Avoid unnecessary words where a less verbose version would be equally clear
rules.notes: Using a sentence with an active subject would make the text much easier to understand

rules.description: Instead of diligently translating each word, try to express the meaning of the source text in Finnish
rules.notes: Translating literally without paying attention to Finnish linguistic conventions and idioms can result in sentences that are hard to understand

rules.section: Terminology and Word Choice - Technical Terms
rules.description: The RingCentral Glossary is your first and binding source for technical terms

rules.section: Terminology and Word Choice - Technical Terms
rules.description: For terms not listed in the glossary stick to Microsoft standard terminology where applicable

rules.section: Terminology and Word Choice - Technical Terms
rules.description: Use available online resources like Wikipedia, administrator web pages, large IT vendor portals etc.
rules.notes: In case of doubt check back with your PM or Language Lead

rules.description: Finnish capitalization differs from English. Follow Finnish rules
When referring to UI elements, always capitalize the first word
The first word following a colon should not be capitalized in Finnish, unless there are more than one sentence after the colon. In that case, the first word should be capitalized
In sentences and headings, only capitalize the first word

Even if titles and headings are fully capitalized in English, follow the Finnish rules. An exception to this are legal texts, where capitalization conveys a specific meaning and must be retained in the translation
Product names should follow source capitalization
Use lowercase for example in months, days, nationalities and currencies
Run the spell-checker when you have completed your translation. If possible, copy the translated word into Word to be able to use the grammar checker too
Brand name tags need to be separated from the preceding or following text with a space
English documents often include double spaces after full stops. Run a controlled search and replace them by single spaces in Finnish

Use non-breaking spaces to prevent certain expressions to be separated at the end of a line, such as people's names, numbers and units, software names and versions, etc. To obtain such a space, press Ctrl + Shift + Space bar in MS Word or use Alt+0160. Do not use non-breaking spaces in software files
Follow the Finnish punctuation rules as described here or in other Finnish reference materials
A full stop is used at the end of a sentence or after many abbreviations
Acronyms and units of measurement are not followed by a full stop
The full stop is used as a separator in version numbers and chapter numbers
Do not use full stops after headings and titles, after columns in a table, or after captions used with illustrations, tables etc., unless the expression considered is a full sentence
Try to avoid over-using colons, especially in marketing related content
Use colons to introduce lists and at the end of a sentence that introduces a procedure
If a colon is followed by more text, the first word after the colon is not capitalized, if the item following the colon is a single sentence

Use the semicolon to link two connected thoughts in the same sentence; to separate items in a series in running text, especially phrases containing commas; or to add emphasis. Semicolons should also be closed up to the preceding word

Ellipses are made up of three dots only. There is a space after the dots. Use for omitted material or for a longer pause than a dash. It is advisable to use the ellipses mark (Alt + 0133) instead of typing three dots
When ellipsis dots are used in English, they should normally be used in Finnish as well
En-dashes (alt + 0150) are used to punctuate sentences in a similar way to colons, commas or brackets, for example to separate a piece of additional information. It should have a space on both sides
En-dashes (alt + 0150) are used to join coordinate or contrasting pairs and as shorthand for the word 'to'
Do not use a hyphen as an en-dash. Em-dashes should not be used in Finnish
Use round brackets for further explications which would otherwise disrupt the sentence. The first letter is a lower case unless the sentence in parentheses is an independent sentence
rules.example_correct: Kumoa tämä asetus (mikäli mahdollista) ja ota yhteyttä järjestelmänvalvojaan

rules.description: Use round brackets when adding the full form of an acronym
The optional area code of phone numbers is written between brackets
Square brackets are used for indicating variables or for indicating additional information within round brackets
NEVER use straight single quote for variables
Doubly curly quotation marks, right quotes (""), are used by default. The opening and closing quotation marks are identical, unlike in English
Single quotation marks are normally only used inside double quotation marks
In English, file names and other variables are often placed between single quotation marks. In Finnish they are not used, unless it is unclear where the name ends
rules.example_correct: Siirry Asetukset-valikkoon, Valitse "Peruuta" ja sulje ikkuna

rules.description: Use the same format in your translation as in the original text for bulleted lists
The sentence introducing the bulleted list ends with a colon. Each part of the enumeration starts with a capital letter and ends with a period (or question mark)
rules.example_correct: Seuraavat tapaukset ovat mahdollisia: ● Palvelin on verkossa. ● Palvelin on verkon ulkopuolella.

rules.description: If the sentence introducing the bulleted list is a complete sentence, it is followed by a colon. If the list items complement it, there should be a full stop at the end of the last list item. If there is a more free association with the introducing sentence and the list items, the full stop can be omitted
rules.example_correct: Palvelin voi sijaita seuraavissa paikoissa: ● verkossa ● verkon ulkopuolella.

rules.section: Punctuation - Bulleted lists - Parts of sentences
rules.description: If the list consists of phrases or parts of sentences, then the sentence introducing the bulleted list does not end in a colon. Each list item starts with a lowercase letter and there is no end punctuation. Only the last item in the bulleted list gets a full stop
rules.example_correct: Ohjelmisto on asennettava, jos ● palvelin on verkossa ● palvelin on verkon ulkopuolella ● palvelinta voi käyttää mobiililaitteilla.

rules.description: Do not use abbreviations in body text. Expressions such as 'mukaan lukien' and 'esimerkiksi' must be written in full instead of being abbreviated
Abbreviations are only used when absolutely necessary, except for units or when there are space issues. They can be used freely in text inside brackets
Acronyms should not be used excessively, so please consider writing out the full expression, if the acronym is not likely to be familiar to the reader
When an acronym first occurs in a text, it should be written out and then followed by the acronym in brackets. After that, you can use the acronym
Numbers should be written in full when used as nouns
Numbers should be written in full when appearing alone and representing simple quantities, usually the numbers up to 9
Dates, large numbers, ages, document references (paragraph, page, etc.) should be written as numbers. Replace the decimal point by a comma and the thousand separator by a non-breaking space
Avoid mixing numerical and alphabetical forms
Finland uses the metric system. Pounds, miles, inches, feet and gallons have no legal meaning and are to be avoided, where possible
Units of measure should be separated from the preceding number by a non-breaking space
Monitor sizes are always expressed in inches and represent an exception to the metric system rule
The percent sign is %, preceded with a non-breaking space
Default Short Date Format: d.m.yyyy - the month (without leading zero), the day (without leading zero) and the year are displayed in numbers

Default Long Date Format: dddd d mmmm yyyy - the names of the weekdays are optional, there is a full stop after the day, and the name of the month is spelled out in full, inflected as '-kuuta'. The years are written using four digits. The names of the workdays and months start with a lower case
There are no approved abbreviations for months, so please consider writing the month in numbers instead, if applicable
The names of the months should be: tammikuu, helmikuu, maaliskuu, huhtikuu, toukokuu, kesäkuu, heinäkuu, elokuu, syyskuu, lokakuu, marraskuu, joulukuu
rules.example_correct: tammikuu, helmikuu, maaliskuu, huhtikuu, toukokuu, kesäkuu, heinäkuu, elokuu, syyskuu, lokakuu, marraskuu, joulukuu

rules.description: The names of the weekdays, in extended and abbreviated forms should be: maanantai ma, tiistai ti, keskiviikko ke, torstai to, perjantai pe, lauantai la, sunnuntai su
rules.example_correct: maanantai ma, tiistai ti, keskiviikko ke, torstai to, perjantai pe, lauantai la, sunnuntai su

rules.description: In a general context, abbreviation klo is preferred. Please use an en dash to express a time range
Abbreviations concerning units of time should not be followed by a full stop. Please use a non-breaking space between the number and the unit

The symbol h is also common in general language for speed indications, where the unit is km/h. No full stop is used after this abbreviation, except for occurrences where it is the last word in a sentence
Currency amounts can be expressed in two ways: 23,50 € or EUR 23,50. The first variant with the Euro sign is preferred. There should be a non-breaking space between the number and the currency symbol

Application/product names are often trademarked and are therefore rarely translated. Before translating any application, product, or feature name, please verify that it is in fact translatable and not protected in any way
Unless otherwise indicated, you will use trademark and registered trademark symbols only on the first mention in the translated version of your content

According to Finnish guidelines, trademark symbol is added after the trademarked name, if it appears on its own or is used as a part of a compound. If the trademarked name is inflected, the symbol will be placed at the end of the word

For third party trademarks and products mentioned in RingCentral content, please conduct research to determine whether an official, approved trademarked translated version is available before using as in source
For product names from third parties, it's correct to follow the localized equivalence in official websites if there's one

rules.section: Miscellaneous - Copyright Information, Footer, Publication Dates
rules.description: Copyright notices tend to be standard texts. Make sure you receive instructions on whether an official and approved translated version is available
rules.example_correct: ©2015 RingCentral, Inc. Kaikki oikeudet pidätetään. RingCentral, RingCentral Office, RingCentral Meetings ja RingCentral-logo ovat RingCentral, Inc:n rekisteröityjä tavaramerkkejä. Tässä asiakirjassa käytetyt kolmansien osapuolten merkit ja logot ovat omistajiensa tavaramerkkejä.

rules.description: Whenever source files include references to URLs, translators should query if said link has a local version, and modify the document accordingly. If not, the relevant URL will be left as it appears in the source English text
If software has been translated, please match the relevant translation memory or string list provided with the translation package
If software has not been translated, please use bilingual format, i.e. English first, followed by Finnish in brackets. Place the translation outside the formatting
When Microsoft terminology or Microsoft UI items are quoted, please refer to Microsoft approved terminology for your language on the MS Portal
Translate country and city names based on your country/region's conventions
Localize address formats, if necessary, based on your country/region's conventions
If source only includes toll free numbers and US numbers, please add an international country code to the local number for international users
rules.example_correct: RingCentral, Inc. (Belmont/HQ) 20 Davis Drive, Belmont, California 94402, USA Puhelin: 888-528-RING (7464) tai +1 650 472 4100
